summary: Read-only trace found only one current bridge open path in the allowed files: bot_core.py receives intents from core.model_bridge.get_approved_intents() and then calls check_entry_gates() before open_position(...). In current on-disk code, failed gate checks already continue before the open. The contradictory live opens for trades 241/243/244 therefore are not explained by a second open_position path inside the scoped files and are most consistent with runtime divergence/stale running code at the time those trades opened.
verification_evidence: bot_core.py imports get_approved_intents from core.model_bridge and the inspected bridge-entry section shows a single open_position(...) call path guarded by check_entry_gates().
In current bot_core.py, if _gate_ok is false, the code logs BRIDGE GATE REJECT, appends loop_guard_reasons, and immediately continue's before reaching open_position(...).
No separate local-origination path remains in the inspected bridge-entry area to explain sub-0.60 opens.
Trade DB rows for ids 241, 243, 244 confirm real opens with low confidences (0.3863, 0.3279, 0.3769).
Available bot log on disk was insufficient to reconstruct exact surrounding open-line chronology for those trade ids in a trustworthy way from the scoped sources.
root_cause_conclusion: Most likely runtime divergence from on-disk code, not a second currently visible open path in the allowed files. The current on-disk bridge path already rejects-and-continues on failed confidence gates, so the previously observed sub-0.60 opens likely came from stale running code or another transient runtime state that is no longer represented on disk.
recommended_next_fix_target: Runtime/version traceability on the live bot path, or a task that captures exact runtime code identity and gate/open event chronology at the moment a low-confidence trade opens. A blind logic patch inside the currently allowed files is not justified by the trace.
